Title: Enrichment of tomato fruit with health-promoting anthocyanins by expression of select transcription factors

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MicroTom; Money Maker; Ailsa Craig; VF36
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: hydrophilic antioxidant activity; associated with protection against cancers, cardiovascular disease, age-related degenerative disease, inflammation, visual decline, obesity and diabetes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit peel; pericarp; inner flesh; initially vascular tissue
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with Ros1 as a MYB-bHLH regulatory combination to induce anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL; 4CL; C4H; CHS; CHI; F3H; F3'H; F3'5'H; DFR; ANS; 3-GT; RT; putative anthocyanin acyltransferase; GST; putative anthocyanin transporter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates almost all genes required for anthocyanin biosynthesis, side-chain modification, and vacuolar transport; strongly induces PAL and F3H expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: fruit ripening context via fruit-specific E8 promoter in transgenic tomato
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Interacts with Ros1 to induce anthocyanin biosynthesis in snapdragon flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit-specific expression during ripening, visible from late mature green stage and intensifying after onset consistent with E8 promoter pattern
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis; phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway activation; anthocyanin modification and transport
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong purple pigmentation and high anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit when coexpressed with Ros1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the decisive Del/Ros1 regulatory pair required to activate a broad anthocyanin program and achieve previously unreported high anthocyanin levels in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic fruit-specific coexpression with Ros1; anthocyanin quantification; HPLC profiling; suppression subtractive hybridization; northern blot; microarray; PAL activity assay; antioxidant capacity assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Del from snapdragon, coexpressed with Ros1 in tomato fruit, induced intense purple coloration in peel and flesh. 2. The Del/Ros1 combination activated almost all anthocyanin biosynthetic genes plus modification and transport genes. 3. The combination strongly increased PAL transcript abundance and PAL activity, contributing to high anthocyanin accumulation and elevated hydrophilic antioxidant capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening stage; fruit-specific transgene expression under E8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious vegetative growth abnormalities reported; stems and leaves showed no abnormal anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a regulatory engineering module to biofortify tomato and potentially other crops with anthocyanins; should be deployed with matching MYB partner and fruit-specific expression to avoid off-target effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper evaluates Del function only in combination with Ros1, not separately; specificity relative to maize Lc/C1 is emphasized as a key reason for success. Omics data include suppression subtractive hybridization and tomato oligonucleotide microarray.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: suppression subtractive hybridization; DNA microarray ArrayExpress array A-MEXP-1417, experiment E-MEXP-1830

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MicroTom; Money Maker; Ailsa Craig; VF36
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: hydrophilic antioxidant activity; associated with protection against cancers, cardiovascular disease, age-related degenerative disease, inflammation, visual decline, obesity and diabetes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit peel; pericarp; inner flesh; initially vascular tissue
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with Del as a MYB-bHLH regulatory combination to induce anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL; 4CL; C4H; CHS; CHI; F3H; F3'H; F3'5'H; DFR; ANS; 3-GT; RT; putative anthocyanin acyltransferase; GST; putative anthocyanin transporter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates almost all genes required for anthocyanin biosynthesis, side-chain modification, and vacuolar transport; induces F3H and genes enabling delphinidin/petunidin-type anthocyanin formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: fruit ripening context via fruit-specific E8 promoter in transgenic tomato
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Interacts with Del to induce anthocyanin biosynthesis in snapdragon flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit-specific expression during ripening, visible from late mature green stage and intensifying after onset consistent with E8 promoter pattern
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis; phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway activation; anthocyanin modification and transport
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong purple pigmentation and high anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit when coexpressed with Del
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the decisive Del/Ros1 regulatory pair required to activate a broad anthocyanin program and achieve previously unreported high anthocyanin levels in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic fruit-specific coexpression with Del; anthocyanin quantification; HPLC profiling; suppression subtractive hybridization; northern blot; microarray; PAL activity assay; antioxidant capacity assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ros1 from snapdragon, coexpressed with Del in tomato fruit, enabled very high anthocyanin accumulation in peel and flesh. 2. The Del/Ros1 combination induced broad expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic, modification, and transport genes. 3. This resulted in ~2.83 mg anthocyanin per g fresh weight in the strongest line and a threefold increase in hydrophilic antioxidant capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening stage; fruit-specific transgene expression under E8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious vegetative growth abnormalities reported; stems and leaves showed no abnormal anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a regulatory engineering module to biofortify tomato and likely other crops with anthocyanins; should be paired with compatible bHLH factor and tissue-specific promoter for strong flux activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper evaluates Ros1 function only in combination with Del, not separately; the authors argue that Del/Ros1 specificity is superior to maize Lc/C1 in tomato. Omics data include suppression subtractive hybridization and tomato oligonucleotide microarray.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: suppression subtractive hybridization; DNA microarray ArrayExpress array A-MEXP-1417, experiment E-MEXP-1830
